# py-maidr

> py-maidr is a Python library that makes matplotlib, seaborn, and plotly data visualizations accessible to blind and low-vision users through sonification, braille, text, and AI-powered conversational modalities.

## Key Links
- Documentation: https://py.maidr.ai/
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/maidr/
- GitHub: https://github.com/xability/py-maidr
- Examples (Matplotlib/Seaborn): https://py.maidr.ai/examples.html
- Examples (Plotly): https://py.maidr.ai/examples-plotly.html
- API Reference: https://py.maidr.ai/api/

## Overview
maidr (pronounced "mader") is an accessible framework for multimodal data representation. It monkey-patches matplotlib, seaborn, and plotly at import time so users just `import maidr` and call `maidr.show(plot)` to generate interactive HTML with sonification, braille, text, and AI chat support. Supported plot types: bar, stacked bar, dodged bar, histogram, KDE, line, multi-line, box, violin, heatmap, scatter, regression, candlestick, multi-panel, and facet plots. Works in Jupyter, Colab, VS Code, Streamlit, Shiny, and Quarto.
